name: Pat's Favorite White Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. In a large bowl, combine flour, sugar, baking powder, and 1/8 teaspoon salt.
3. Add buttermilk, shortening (butter or margarine), and vanilla. Mix on low speed for 30 seconds, scraping the sides of the bowl.
4. Beat on medium to high speed for 2 minutes, scraping the bowl occasionally.
5. Add egg whites and beat for an additional 2 minutes, scraping the bowl.
6. Divide batter evenly between two greased and floured 9-inch round baking pans.
7. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for 30-35 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
8. Cool on wire racks for 10 minutes.
9. Loosen edges of cakes from the pans, then turn out onto racks to cool completely.
NOTE: To make marble cake, reserve one-third of the batter and divide it between the pans in dollops. Melt one square unsweetened or semi-sweet chocolate over boiling water or in a microwave. Stir into the remaining batter until blended. Spoon some chocolate batter into each pan and gently swirl to create marbled effect before baking as directed.
